channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Labrinth Addresses Why His Music Won’t Be in ‘Euphoria’ Season 3: ‘I Don’t Let People Treat Me Like S—t’
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to Trending, the podcast that brings you the latest news from the world of entertainment. I'm your host, and today we're talking about the hit HBO series Euphoria and the surprising news that Labrinth will no longer be involved in the show's music. Let's dive in."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Labrinth, the 37-year-old British singer and producer, took to Instagram to explain why his music won't be featured in season 3 of Euphoria, which premieres on Sunday. In a statement, he wrote that he decided to remove his music from the show, citing a desire to prioritize his own artistic vision and not be treated poorly by the industry."
[Short music transition]
Host: "This news comes after it was announced that Oscar winner Hans Zimmer will be serving as the sole composer for the show's upcoming eight episodes. Labrinth had previously collaborated with Zimmer on the show's music, but it seems that their working relationship has come to an end. Labrinth has been an integral part of Euphoria's sound since its debut in 2019, and his departure is sure to be felt."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Euphoria creator Sam Levinson has spoken out about the situation, calling Labrinth an 'incredible collaborator' and expressing his admiration for the artist's work. Levinson also explained that Hans Zimmer is the right choice for season 3, citing the show's complex storytelling and the need for a composer who can bring each character's storyline to life."
[Short music transition]
Host: "Labrinth is no stranger to speaking his mind, and this isn't the first time he's taken to Instagram to express his frustration with the industry. Earlier this month, he criticized his record label and Euphoria in a now-deleted post. Despite this, Labrinth is still set to perform at the 2026 Coachella festival, so fans can still look forward to seeing him live on stage."
[Outro music plays]
Host: "That's all for today's episode of Trending. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll see you next time."
